description: We carry out sentences given by the courts, in custody and the community, and rehabilitate people in our care through education and employment. HMPPS is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice .

details.body:
We carry out sentences given by the courts, in custody and the community, and rehabilitate people in our care through education and employment.

HMPPS is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Working in a prison and the probation service is a truly unique experience – extraordinary jobs, done by people like you.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Find your role at HMPPS

ordered_featured_links.title: Find a prison in England and Wales

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use. Find out About our services.

details.body:
The Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function (GFF) co-leads and develops the community of finance professionals across government, to help to maintain the highest standards of financial management and ensures that public money is spent efficiently and effectively.

Key responsibilities include:

  • setting and delivering a GFF strategy for the development of the Government Finance Function
  • overseeing the professional development and performance of the GFF, including the Government Finance Academy curriculum and the annual continuing professional development plan
  • supporting departments’ talent management, succession planning and recruitment for the most senior financial roles in government

The Joint-Head of the GFF is accountable to Parliamentary select committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, for the continuing improvement of financial management standards, skills and expertise in government.

role.title: Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: James McEwen - Joint-Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body:
The Director General leads the COO Group, supporting the MOJ in delivering its priorities.

The COO Group includes:

  • Commercial & Contract Management
  • Finance
  • People Function
  • Project Delivery
  • Property
  • Security and Information Services

role_appointments.title: James McEwen - Chief Operating Officer and Director General of COO Group

role.title: Director General Chief Executive Officer HMPPS

role_appointments.title: James McEwen - Director General Chief Executive HMPPS
Sarah Coccia - Chief Operating Officer Prisons

details.body:
• leading the Probation Service in England and Wales, an organisation that supervises offenders in the community on court orders, on licence from prison and subject to Post-Sentence Supervision
  • driving excellent practice across the Probation Service, with a particular emphasis on risk management and public protection.
  • ensuring the Probation Service reduces reoffending by delivering the best possible sentence management and interventions.

role_appointments.title: Kim Thornden-Edwards - Chief Probation Officer

details.body:
• providing safe and decent child-centred care for children and young people remanded or sentenced to custody aged 10 to 18 in England and Wales
  • safeguarding children and young people in custody, and creating a supportive learning environment
  • improving the opportunities for children and young people in custody to support successful release and resettlement
  • direct operational delivery of 4 public sector under 18 Young Offender Institutions (YOIs) and oversight of a privately delivered YOI unit in Wales
  • commissioning of services, monitoring and contractual management of placements into Secure Children’s Homes, the Secure Training Centre and the first Secure School
  • strategic development of the Children and Young People’s Secure Estate including the delivery of the Secure School project

role_appointments.title: Alison Clarke - Executive Director, Youth Custody Service
Michelle Jarman-Howe - Chief Operating Officer Prisons
Michelle Jarman-Howe - Director General Operations HMPPS

role.title: Executive Director Public Sector Prisons South

role_appointments.title: Michelle Jarman-Howe - Executive Director Public Sector Prisons South

details.body:
Responsibilities Include:

  • Delivery of residential services in the community and is the SRO for the Reducing Reoffending Delivery Programme.
  • Design and delivery of the new Community Accommodation Service (CAS3), including the expansion and improvement of Approved Premises, delivery of Bail Accommodation and Support Services (BASS) and the new ‘vanguard’ accommodation provision for all people leaving prison without suitable accommodation.
  • Designing the new Prison Education Service as well as ongoing collaborative working with health partners to deliver improved mental health and substance misuse outcomes.
  • Hannah is also the HMPPS Disability Champion.

role.title: Executive Director Reducing Reoffending, Partnerships & Accommodation

role_appointments.title: Hannah Meyer - Executive Director Reducing Reoffending, Partnerships & Accommodation
Hannah Meyer - Executive Director Strategy, Planning & Performance

details.body:
Our non-executives are senior figures from outside the agency who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give guidance and advice to HMPPS leaders
  • support and challenge management on the agency’s strategic direction
  • provide support in monitoring and reviewing progress

role_appointments.title: Dame Glenys Stacey - Non-Executive Director, HMPPS

details.body: The Chief Regulator answers to Parliament and also serves as the Chief Executive and Accounting Officer. It’s their role to make sure that the organisation carries out the board’s strategy, and that Ofqual has the resources and structure it needs to do its work effectively. As Accounting Officer to Ofqual, the Chief Regulator is accountable for Ofqual’s deployment of public funds.

role_appointments.title: Dame Glenys Stacey - Chief Regulator, Ofqual

details.body: The Chief Regulator answers to Parliament and also serves as the Chief Executive and Accounting Officer. It’s their role to make sure that the organisation carries out the board’s strategy, and that Ofqual has the resources and structure it needs to do its work effectively. role_appointments.title: Non-Executive Director Mark Lloyd - Non-Executive Director

details.body:
Our Non-executives are senior figures from outside the Agency who bring a diverse mix of expertise and skills from across the public and private sector. They all:

  • give advice to ministers and officials on the operational and delivery implications of policy proposals
  • provide independent support, guidance and challenge on the progress and implementation of the organisation’s strategic direction
  • advise on performance and monitor implementation of business plans
